about-igem.name: Join iGEM!
about-igem.title: What is iGEM?
texts: International Genetically Engineered Machines (iGEM) is an international student competition founded by MIT focused in the fields of synthetic biology. High schools, colleges, and entrepreneurial teams from all across the globe come together every year to compete in iGEM. Ambitious students dare to change the world by creating biological systems to that have the potential to solve problems in their communities or in the world. iGEM is a great opportunity for students with different backgrounds to come together and pursue science research and engineering!
If you're interested in science research, iGEM is for you! Come to room 2517 for our interest meeting on Wednesday, September 10th after school or Thursday, September 11th at 9 AM.

our-projects.name: Our Projects
our-projects.title: Our Projects
texts:
This year, we will work on developing non-toxic treatments for the innate immune system. Through a decade of study, the innate immune system has been revealed to demonstrate a previously unexpected paradigm-- capability of memory. However, this memory has been shown to be the underlying cause of chronic low-grade inflammation, chronic expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and innate immune system imbalance in diseases like obesity, atherosclerosis, sepsis, and even some types of cancer. This type of "memory" is characterized as an evolutionary response to acute injury, and it primary functions through the toll-like receptor 4 intracellular pathway (TLR4). Recently, studies have demonstrated that a drug known as 4-phenylbutyric acid (4-PBA), a drug previously used to treat urinary diseases, is capable of a type "reprogramming" by inhibiting the TLR4 pathway. However, 4-PBA and similar phenylbutyrates have also been demonstrated to cause acute hepatotoxicity, exacerbate bipolar and related disorders, cause amenorrhea, cause extreme appetite loss, cause neurotoxicity, etc.

This year, we will research alternatives to 4-PBA by looking for different molecules with similar attributes and potentials to inhibit the TLR4 pathway, and subsequently running tests on them. Phase 1 will consist of utilization of predictive software to simulate interactions of the molecular alternatives, and Phase 2 will consist of successful candidates of Phase 1 being tested on murine macrophage cultures through western blot and ELISA assays. If Phase 2 demonstrates success, we will look into potentially performing a Phase 3 involving flow cytometry to gain more detailed insight.

This is our most ambitious project yet, so stay tuned and support us throughout the year!

Readings: https://doi.org/10.1161/CIRCRESAHA.124.325023, https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adf6582, image courtesy of https://li.biol.vt.edu/

This year, we will work on developing an mRNA vaccine for lyme disease. Although mRNA vaccines have been thought to be more efficient than traditional vaccines for many years, only in 2020 was the first mRNA vaccine approved by the FDA (for COVID-19). We hope to innovate in the vaccine field by creating an mRNA vaccine designed to combat against lyme disease, building on a previous lyme mRNA vaccine created by University of Pennsylvania in 2023.

We will create a bacteria that will mimic insect specific symbiotic bacteria (Wolbachia) to combat a vector-borne disease (lyme disease), we will use Drosophila Melanogaster (fruit flies) as our insect model and proof of principle. To create our mimic, we’ll add surface protein genes that are found in Wolbachia to BL21 or DH5-alpha cells (we will confirm the surface proteins by ELISA using antibodies against Wolbachia surface protein). Once our mimic is well established through several fruit fly generations (we will confirm this by PCR), we will add a fluorescent gene that will be activated by products generated by the Lyme disease bacteria (we will first do in-vitro studies to make sure it works). If this works, we will do the same work on ticks which are the natural vectors for lyme disease and we will add another gene that will inactivate the lyme bacteria. If our insects are released in the wild, they will mate and pass on our mimic bacteria. If they come in contact with the lyme disease, our ticks will generate the fluorescent marker which flags the insect as a lyme disease active organism, making them easier to identify, and at the same time, it will inactivate the lyme bacteria. If this works, we will be able to use this technology with other variants of lymes disease to combat Lymes and its side effects.

our-team.name: Our Team
our-team.title: Our Team
texts:
The Independence High School iGEM team is a biology team composed of around 20 members in all years of high school. The team is in its first year competing at the MIT-run international competition, so the aspiring team relies much on the support of the community. The members of our team are all highly devoted towards gaining a better understanding in the fields of synthetic biology. We strive to create and innovate in directions that will benefit society.
 In the process, we are developing outstanding future researchers and biologists that will go on to have successful careers in any field of science they pursue.

progress-and-updates-blog.name: Progress & Updates Blog
progress-and-updates-blog.title: Progress & Updates Blog
texts: Happy September! As the school year starts back up, so are we! Our project idea is in its final drafts, and our interest meeting is just around the corner. We look forward to a successful year of iGEM!

JUNE 2024:
Happy June! STEM day was a huge success! Both the kids and the volunteers had a wonderful time learning about and teaching science. Looking ahead, we are planning to meet in the summer to further our progress on our recent project, and current sponsor Mr. Saavedra will sadly be leaving us.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

MAY 2024:
Happy May! All of our new members have learned so much over these past few months about the basics of biotechnology and common protocols performed in the lab. Now, we prepare for our biggest fundraising event of the year, STEM day. Be sure to come check it out to participate in some cool science activities!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

JANUARY 2024: 
Happy new year! Our club has been rapidly growing, from what felt like a few kids to a 20 person team with specialized departments! Many of us are fairly inexperienced, so we are all learning so much about biotechnology. We know that this year will be monumental in our club's history!

sponsors.name: Sponsors
sponsors.title: Sponsors
texts: Thank you for taking the time to look at our Sponsors Page. As a first year school, it is very hard raising enough funds to compete in the iGEM competition. That is why we rely on companies and businesses for financial support. For more information on the program, budget, and on how your company can get involved, see the Independence iGEM Letter of Support below.
Independence High School iGEM Letter of Support (OUTDATED)

stem-day.name: STEM Day 2026
stem-day.title: Stem Day
texts: STEM day is an event run by Independence High School's iGEM team that focuses on bringing fun STEM activities to young minds. Make your way through a series of 20+ STEM-related stations and collect a free prize at the end! We got over 300+ visitors last year and would love to make this year's STEM day even bigger!
